articles.title: Kedarnath Yatra: Helicopter Services Halted At Key Points, Orange Alert Issued For Two Days - curlytales.com
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
The Kedarnath Dham Yatra is currently facing disruption due to adverse weather conditions. An orange alert has been issued in Uttarakhand, and helicopter services have been halted for pilgrims. Officials are closely tracking the situation, and pilgrims are advised to take extra precautions. Here’s more on this.
Kedarnath Yatra Helicopter Services Halted Due To Bad Weather
Weather conditions are currently bad at Kedarnath Dham in Uttarakhand. According to Times Now, helicopter services have been temporarily suspended not only at the Dham but also in Phata, Sonprayag, Sersi, and Guptkashi. A two-day orange alert has also been announced, warning of heavy rain, hailstorms, and lightning across Chamoli, Rudraprayag, Uttarkashi, and Pithoragarh.
Authorities are now on high alert. They are closely tracking conditions across the route and up to the Kedarnath Temple. Pilgrims have been advised to take extra precautions. Sector magistrates, SDRF teams, and police personnel are on standby to monitor the situation.

Pilgrims Advised To Check Real-Time Updates Before Starting Journey
According to Times Now, the weather is also getting colder at Kedarnath Dham. This is why the administration is urging travellers to check weather updates before starting their journey. Moreover, pilgrims are being strictly told to follow official guidelines to ensure a smooth and safe pilgrimage.
If you are planning to begin your journey, pack properly while keeping the changing weather in mind. Carry extra layers, raincoats, proper trekking gear, and other essentials. Authorities will resume helicopter services once the weather returns to normal. Until then, keep an eye on real-time updates. The doors to the Kedarnath Temple opened for the 2026 season on April 22, following traditional Vedic rituals. The closing date this year is expected to be around October-November.

Are you planning to visit Kedarnath Temple this year? What will you prefer for the yatra, the traditional trek or the helicopter service?
Cover Image Courtesy: Canva Pro/ Andrey and ÇİĞDEM EYCE
For more such snackable content, interesting discoveries and the latest updates on food, travel and experiences in your city, download the Curly Tales App. Download HERE. First Published: April 30, 2026 11:09 AMFAQs
Where is the Kedarnath Temple?
Kedarnath Temple is located in the Rudraprayag district of Uttarakhand in the Himalayas.
Are there helicopter services at Kedarnath Temple?
Yes, helicopter services are available at the Kedarnath Temple. They offer a quick alternative to the long trek.
For how long does the Kedarnath Temple stay open for devotees?
Kedarnath Temple remains open for around six months a year. It typically opens in late April and closes around October-November.

articles.title: Weather change in Badrinath brings relief during heatwave - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Chamoli district in Uttarakhand experienced a sudden change in weather on April 25, as unexpected rainfall provided relief to residents after several days of intense heat. This shift notably eased the daily conditions for locals coping with high temperatures.
In the sacred town of Badrinath Dham, light rain in the afternoon caused a significant drop in temperature, creating a refreshing coolness. Additionally, light snowfall was recorded on the higher peaks surrounding Badrinath, further improving the weather conditions. Pilgrims visiting for the darshan of Lord Badri Vishal described the scenic change as a special blessing.
Amid the ongoing heatwave, the rain and snowfall generated enthusiasm among devotees, who expressed joy at witnessing this rare natural display at the high-altitude Himalayan shrine.
Earlier, on April 23 at 6:15 AM, the portals of Shri Badrinath Dham were opened to devotees following traditional rituals and the chanting of Vedic mantras. The temple was decorated to mark the start of the pilgrimage season.
Uttarakhand Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami stated in a post on X, “Today, with full rites and rituals and the chanting of Vedic mantras, the gates of the sacred Badrinath Dham, the earthly Vaikunth, will be opened for devotees. May the grace of Lord Badrinath Vishal bring happiness, peace, and prosperity into the lives of all devotees. May this sacred occasion bring a message of spiritual energy, faith, and a positive beginning for everyone. A hearty welcome and warm greetings to all of you for the Holy Char Dham Yatra – 2026.”

articles.title: Uttarakhand: बारिश, बर्फबारी ने दी गर्मी से राहत, चमोली के ऊंचाई वाले क्षेत्रों में गिरी बर्फ, मसरी में ठंडक - Amar Ujala
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
Uttarakhand: बारिश, बर्फबारी ने दी गर्मी से राहत, चमोली के ऊंचाई वाले क्षेत्रों में गिरी बर्फ, मसरी में ठंडक
प्रदेश में एक बार फिर मौसम का मिजाज बदला। चमोली जिले के ऊंचाई वाले क्षेत्रों में जहां बर्फबारी हुई वहीं मसूरी में बारिश ने ठंडक का अहसास कराया।
विस्तार
उत्तराखंड के चमोली और मसूरी में मौसम ने करवट बदली और बारिश बर्फबारी ने लोगों को गर्मी से राहत दिलाई। गोपेश्वर में दोपहर में कुछ देर के लिए ओलावृष्टि हुई। बारिश, बर्फबारी से लोगों को गर्मी से राहत मिली है।
वहीं पहाड़ों की रानी मसूरी में जमकर बारिश हुई। बारिश के साथ ओले भी पड़े। मौसम में ठंडक लौट आई। सड़क की नालियां बंद होने से बरसात का पानी सड़क पर बहने लगा। मौसम विभाग ने अगले 24 से 48 घंटों के भीतर राज्य के कई क्षेत्रों में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश होने की संभावना जताई थी। इसके साथ ही करीब 40 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाएं चलने के आसार हैं। कुछ जिलों में बारिश का दौर आगामी पांच मई तक जारी रह सकता है।

मौसम विभाग ने विशेष रूप से देहरादून, टिहरी, उत्तरकाशी, रुद्रप्रयाग, चमोली, बागेश्वर, पिथौरागढ़ और नैनीताल जनपदों के लिए अलर्ट जारी किया है। इन पहाड़ी क्षेत्रों में अगले 24 से 48 घंटों के दौरान ओलावृष्टि और तेज बारिश की संभावना जताई गई है। अचानक बदलते मौसम को देखते हुए किसानों और आमजन को सतर्क रहने की सलाह दी गई है।
मौसम निदेशक सीएस तोमर ने बताया कि आगामी 24 से 48 घंटों में प्रदेशभर में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश दर्ज की जा सकती है। उन्होंने यह भी बताया कि साप्ताहिक पूर्वानुमान के अनुसार पांच मई तक कुछ जनपदों में हल्की बारिश का दौर जारी रह सकता है। मौसम विभाग ने लोगों से सतर्क रहने और जारी दिशा-निर्देशों का पालन करने की अपील की है।
